description: After changing settings and closing the settings dialog, reopening it from the menu bar triggers an accessibility permission prompt. This is wrong — accessibility is already granted. The issue is that check_accessibility() is likely being called again on the code path that opens the settings window, or the act of bringing the app to Regular activation policy re-triggers the check. The fix should ensure check_accessibility() only runs once at startup, not on every window show.
title: Spurious accessibility prompt when reopening settings
